summary: Jeff Atwood shares a reusable VB.NET Shell class he created for executing command line utilities from within ASP.NET applications and capturing their output. The class wraps System.Diagnostics.Process and handles multithreaded output/error capture, timeouts, and cleanup. He notes the current implementation is synchronous but could be extended to support asynchronous operation with events for long-running processes. The post is primarily a code share with minimal commentary, serving as a practical utility for developers facing similar challenges.
key_points: The Shell class encapsulates Process execution with output/error capture, timeout handling, and proper resource disposal
Separate threads are spawned to read stdout and stderr to prevent deadlocks during process execution
The class supports both synchronous blocking execution and optional threaded launch for async scenarios
Proper cleanup is implemented via IDisposable pattern and thread abortion on completion or timeout
The WorkingDirectory property must be set if executables aren't in the default system path
money_quotes: I wrote a little multithreaded .Process wrapper class to encapsulate this behavior. It's nothing magical, but it is handy for these scenarios.
Don't forget to set the .WorkingDirectory if your executables aren't in the default path.

If you have long running console processes, you might want to make this class asynchronous (e.g., non-blocking) and raise events for things like console lines being written, command terminating, etcetera.
key_insight: Wrapping command line execution in a reusable class with proper threading, timeout handling, and resource cleanup makes integrating external tools into .NET applications significantly cleaner and more reliable.
